jokes.text: Um, is it me or does it smell like updog in here?
jokes.setup: Jim sets up the classic 'updog' trap with innocent question about smell
jokes.punchline: Waiting for someone to ask 'What's updog?' so he can respond with 'Nothing much, what's up with you?'
jokes.explanation: Classic setup executed well, though the updog joke itself is very familiar

jokes.text: What's updog? / Nothing much. What's up with you?
jokes.setup: Victim falls for the updog trap
jokes.punchline: Jim delivers the classic punchline
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of a classic joke structure, very economical

jokes.text: Oh, wow. I walked right into that.
jokes.setup: Character realizes they fell for the obvious trap
jokes.punchline: Self-aware acknowledgment of being fooled
jokes.explanation: Nice self-aware moment that acknowledges the joke structure

jokes.text: Hey, Stanley. Is that jacket made of updog? / I'm on the phone.
jokes.setup: Jim tries the updog joke on Stanley
jokes.punchline: Stanley completely ignores him, focused on his phone call
jokes.explanation: Perfect Stanley characterization - completely uninterested in Jim's antics

jokes.text: What flavor coffee is that, updog? / What's that? / I don't know. Nothing. What's up with you? / Huh? No. Damn it.
jokes.setup: Jim tries updog again but fumbles the delivery
jokes.punchline: The victim doesn't fall for it and Jim gets frustrated
jokes.explanation: Shows Jim's persistence and occasional failure with his pranks

jokes.text: Dwight. Hey, is it me, or does this place smell like updog? / What's updog? / Gotcha! / Oh, God! Crap.
jokes.setup: Jim tries updog on Dwight
jokes.punchline: Dwight falls for it completely and has dramatic reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dwight reaction - taking it seriously and being dramatically upset when fooled

jokes.text: But if you do your spring cleaning in January, guess what you don't have to do in the spring? Anything.
jokes.setup: Michael explains his logic for January spring cleaning
jokes.punchline: The obvious conclusion delivered with unwarranted pride
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael logic - obvious conclusion presented as brilliant insight

jokes.text: They say a cluttered desk means a cluttered mind. Well, I say an empty desk means an... Empty mind. No, that's not... No. That's not what I was gonna say.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to twist the common saying about cluttered desks
jokes.punchline: He accidentally proves the opposite point and realizes it mid-sentence
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-defeating logic joke that perfectly captures Michael's character

jokes.text: Could've used some of that famous Hispanic cleaning ethic.
jokes.setup: Michael commenting on Oscar being out sick on cleaning day
jokes.punchline: Casual racist stereotype delivered obliviously
jokes.explanation: Cringe comedy from Michael's casual racism, though relies on offensive stereotypes

jokes.text: Which is exactly how you'd want to sound if you wanted someone to think you were sick. / That's exactly what I was thinking.
jokes.setup: Michael becomes suspicious of Oscar's sick call
jokes.punchline: Dwight immediately agrees with the paranoid logic
jokes.explanation: Good Michael-Dwight dynamic showing their shared paranoid mindset

jokes.text: Because an office can't function efficiently unless people are at their desks doing their jobs.
jokes.setup: Michael explains why investigating Oscar is important
jokes.punchline: Said while he's literally not at his desk doing his job
jokes.explanation: Nice ironic moment showing Michael's lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Man, this must be torture for you.
jokes.setup: Roy commenting on Jim watching Pam talk about wedding plans
jokes.punchline: Roy unknowingly highlighting Jim's pain
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the dramatic irony of the love triangle situation

jokes.text: I confided in the world's worst confidant.
jokes.setup: Jim explains telling Michael his secret
jokes.punchline: Perfectly describes Michael's inability to keep secrets
jokes.explanation: Economical and accurate character assessment that sets up the episode's conflict

jokes.text: And it is why I intend on keeping that secret for as long as I possibly can.
jokes.setup: Michael talks about the importance of keeping Jim's secret
jokes.punchline: The qualifier 'as long as I possibly can' reveals he'll definitely tell
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael characterization - the wording betrays his inability to keep secrets

jokes.text: See the game last night? / Which game? / Any of 'em.
jokes.setup: Michael tries to make small talk with Stanley
jokes.punchline: Reveals he didn't actually watch any games, just wants to talk
jokes.explanation: Great example of Michael's desperate attempt at connection through fake common ground

jokes.text: So, uh, what's the 4-1-1? Any updates on the 'P' situation? / P-A-M... P-A?
jokes.setup: Michael tries to talk in code about Pam
jokes.punchline: Immediately spells out exactly what he's talking about
jokes.explanation: Michael's complete inability to actually keep things secret, even when trying

jokes.text: Peach iced tea. / You're gonna hate it.
jokes.setup: Stanley choosing peach iced tea
jokes.punchline: Kevin's blunt assessment delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kevin delivery - blunt honesty about something trivial

jokes.text: Unfortunately, I spoke to Oscar on the phone. So none of this is useful.
jokes.setup: Dwight explains how to detect lying through body language
jokes.punchline: Realizes the information is completely useless for a phone call
jokes.explanation: Great deadpan delivery of Dwight realizing his expertise is irrelevant

jokes.text: It's grrrrape soda. / Tony the Tiger, you don't hear that much anymore.
jokes.setup: Kevin growls 'grape' like Tony the Tiger
jokes.punchline: Michael's nostalgic observation about the advertising icon
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor about changing advertising landscape

jokes.text: I had asked Michael if I could head up the Oscar investigation, and he said that only Dwight was capable of handling such sensitive material.
jokes.setup: Jim creates a fake secret to distract from the real one
jokes.punchline: The 'secret' is actually complimentary to Dwight
jokes.explanation: Clever misdirection that plays to Dwight's ego while protecting the real secret

jokes.text: Oh, man. You should order milk. Get it?
jokes.setup: Michael at Hooters making inappropriate suggestion
jokes.punchline: The terrible innuendo followed by 'Get it?' shows his lack of subtlety
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Michael's inappropriate behavior, though the joke itself is weak

jokes.text: We're not worthy. We're not worthy.
jokes.setup: Michael sees the Hooters waitress approaching
jokes.punchline: Wayne's World reference showing his awkwardness around women
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that fits Michael's character but isn't particularly clever

jokes.text: I will have a chicken breast. Hold the chicken.
jokes.setup: Michael ordering at Hooters
jokes.punchline: Terrible attempt at innuendo that makes no logical sense
jokes.explanation: The cringe works because it's so poorly executed, perfectly capturing Michael's awkwardness

jokes.text: Sniffling how? / Um, how many different ways are there to gonna sniffle? / Three.
jokes.setup: Dwight interrogating Pam about Oscar's sniffling
jokes.punchline: Dwight's confident assertion that there are exactly three ways to sniffle
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist comedy perfectly suited to Dwight's character - confident expertise about nonsense

jokes.text: Her legs or boobs, or...? / Um, she's easy to talk to, I guess. / Really? She never gets any of my jokes.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Jim about what he likes about Pam
jokes.punchline: Michael's crude reduction vs Jim's genuine answer, then Michael's oblivious response
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Jim's sincerity and Michael's shallow perspective

jokes.text: Happy birthday song at Hooters
jokes.setup: Michael claims it's Jim's birthday to get attention
jokes.punchline: Jim's visible discomfort during the performance
jokes.explanation: Standard cringe scenario elevated by Jim's uncomfortable reactions

jokes.text: What did you guys talk about? / Just, you know, politics. Literature. / I hate you.
jokes.setup: Michael asks what Jim talked to the waitresses about
jokes.punchline: Jim's obviously false highbrow answer and Michael's genuine disappointment
jokes.explanation: Good interplay showing Jim's dry humor and Michael's envy

jokes.text: If I had to, I could clean out my desk in five seconds and nobody would ever know I had ever been here. And I'd forget too.
jokes.setup: Ryan contemplating how little he's invested in the job
jokes.punchline: The additional detail that he'd forget too emphasizes his detachment
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures the temp mindset with dark humor about meaninglessness

jokes.text: I put a cigarette through a freaking quarter. And you know what, Toby? They almost bought from us.
jokes.setup: Michael defending his magic shop purchase as business expense
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous magic trick and obviously false claim about almost making a sale
jokes.explanation: Classic Michael delusion about his business acumen and what impresses clients

jokes.text: He is in love with a girl he works with who's engaged. / Pam?
jokes.setup: Michael trying to justify the Hooters expense
jokes.punchline: Immediately reveals Jim's secret to Toby
jokes.explanation: Perfect execution of Michael's inability to keep secrets - reveals it while trying to justify something else

jokes.text: One time, I suspected an ex-girlfriend of mine of cheating on me. So I tailed her for six nights straight. Turns out she was. With a couple of guys, actually, so. Mystery solved.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining his surveillance experience
jokes.punchline: The casual revelation about multiple guys and matter-of-fact 'mystery solved'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor delivered in Dwight's matter-of-fact style, escalates beyond expectations

jokes.text: Well, the cat's out of the bag. I used to have a crush on Pam. And now I don't. / Riveting.
jokes.setup: Jim's resigned talking head about his secret being revealed
jokes.punchline: His sarcastic self-commentary on his own story
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor with Jim commenting on his own mundane revelation

jokes.text: I told Michael that I had had a crush on you when you first started here. / Oh.
jokes.setup: Jim's awkward confession to Pam
jokes.punchline: Pam's simple 'Oh' reaction and the awkward silence that follows
jokes.explanation: Perfect uncomfortable moment with minimal dialogue carrying maximum emotional weight

jokes.text: So are you gonna be, like, totally awkward around me now? / Oh, yeah. Yeah. / Hope that's okay.
jokes.setup: Pam asking if things will be awkward now
jokes.punchline: Jim's honest admission that yes, it will be awkward
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of typical romantic comedy dialogue - honest about the awkwardness

jokes.text: I don't tell Michael, and in exchange, you owe me one great, big, giant favor redeemable by me at a time and place of my choosing.
jokes.setup: Dwight catching Oscar in his lie
jokes.punchline: The overly formal and dramatic terms for blackmail
jokes.explanation: Classic Dwight pompous formality applied to workplace blackmail

jokes.text: Otherwise, it's just malfeasance for malfeasance's-s sake.
jokes.setup: Dwight explaining why he didn't report Oscar
jokes.punchline: The unnecessarily complex vocabulary and slight stutter on 'sake'
jokes.explanation: Dwight's pretentious vocabulary creates humor through the slight verbal stumble

jokes.text: Oh, I thought that was more on a volunteer basis. / No. That was mandatory. / Oh, I thought it was a volunteer thing.
jokes.setup: Michael asking Creed about organizing the menu book
jokes.punchline: Creed repeating the same excuse despite being corrected
jokes.explanation: Perfect Creed moment showing his complete disconnect from workplace reality

jokes.text: Jim had a crush on me on the booze cruise, or he told you about it on the booze cruise. / Um... okay. / Shut it, Michael. I'm done. That's it, I'm out.
jokes.setup: Pam realizes Michael revealed more than Jim intended
jokes.punchline: Her immediate shutdown of Michael and exit
jokes.explanation: Great escalation as Pam realizes the full scope of Michael's secret-telling

jokes.text: Portrait of a Prostitute. Something... Secrets of a... More Secrets of a Call Girl.
jokes.setup: Michael trying to remember a movie title
jokes.punchline: The fumbling through inappropriate movie titles
jokes.explanation: Typical Michael inappropriate reference delivered with characteristic uncertainty

jokes.text: I don't wanna be Shila. I like being Michael Scott.
jokes.setup: Michael's convoluted explanation about why he can't keep secrets
jokes.punchline: The ridiculous comparison between himself and a movie prostitute character
jokes.explanation: Perfect Michael logic - dramatic comparison to justify his inability to keep secrets
